# submitit insane: mixed executor workflow

Wrap the `submitit` package root, retain executor entry points plus `helpers.run_cmd`, and use a checked-in script to run DebugExecutor, LocalExecutor, and AutoExecutor jobs.

## Why This Case Exists

The top submitit rung should prove that the suite can retain a broad scheduling-oriented wrapper surface while still doing real queued work through several executor flavors.

## Case Shape

- ID: `submitit_root`
- Family: `submitit`
- Difficulty: `insane`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `package`
- Workflow Slice: `hpc`
- Target Kind: `package`
- Upstream Target: `submitit`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/submitit_root/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

- Minimum generated tools: `20`
- Required generated tools: `helpers__run_cmd`, `create_auto_executor`, `create_debug_executor`, `auto_executor_update_parameters`, `create_local_executor`, `local_executor_update_parameters`, `debug_executor_update_parameters`

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the case-study lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=submitit_root
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only submitit_root
```

## Prepared Inputs

- Render `challenges/cases/submitit_root/fixtures/run_mixed_jobs.py` to `{challenge_artifact_dir}/run_mixed_jobs.py` before the workflow runs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/submitit_root/fixtures/run_mixed_jobs.py`

## Workflow

### 1. Launch the checked-in mixed submitit helper script through the root facade

Tool: `helpers__run_cmd`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "args": [
    [
      "{python_executable}",
      "{challenge_artifact_dir}/run_mixed_jobs.py",
      "{challenge_artifact_dir}"
    ]
  ],
  "kwargs": {
    "cwd": "{challenge_artifact_dir}"
  }
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['HELLO', '6', '8']; files are non-empty ['{challenge_artifact_dir}/mixed_summary.json', '{challenge_artifact_dir}/debug_files.txt', '{challenge_artifact_dir}/local_files.txt', '{challenge_artifact_dir}/auto_files.txt'].

## What This Case Proves

- One-shot package ingestion can wrap an HPC-adjacent executor family instead of a single class.
- The generated facade can launch a helper script that performs real DebugExecutor, LocalExecutor, and AutoExecutor submissions.
- The deterministic wrapper path can reach beyond solvers into orchestration tooling while retaining inspectable artifacts.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: wrap an HPC-oriented executor object from a scheduler helper library.
# This keeps the challenge close to real orchestration work without requiring a cluster.
id = "submitit_root"
title = "submitit insane: mixed executor workflow"
tier = "local_full"
style = "package"
slice = "hpc"
family = "submitit"
difficulty = "insane"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `submitit` package root, retain executor entry points plus `helpers.run_cmd`, and use a checked-in script to run DebugExecutor, LocalExecutor, and AutoExecutor jobs."
motivation = "The top submitit rung should prove that the suite can retain a broad scheduling-oriented wrapper surface while still doing real queued work through several executor flavors."
proves = [
  "One-shot package ingestion can wrap an HPC-adjacent executor family instead of a single class.",
  "The generated facade can launch a helper script that performs real DebugExecutor, LocalExecutor, and AutoExecutor submissions.",
  "The deterministic wrapper path can reach beyond solvers into orchestration tooling while retaining inspectable artifacts.",
]

[target]
kind = "package"
value = "submitit"

[probe]
imports = ["submitit"]

[scaffold]
kind = "package"
include_submodules = true
symbol_include_patterns = ["^AutoExecutor$", "^DebugExecutor$", "^LocalExecutor$", "^run_cmd$"]
max_generated_tools = 24

[setup]
[[setup.rendered_files]]
source = "fixtures/run_mixed_jobs.py"
destination = "{challenge_artifact_dir}/run_mixed_jobs.py"

[ingestion]
min_generated_tools = 20
required_tools = [
  "helpers__run_cmd",
  "create_auto_executor",
  "create_debug_executor",
  "auto_executor_update_parameters",
  "create_local_executor",
  "local_executor_update_parameters",
  "debug_executor_update_parameters",
]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Launch the checked-in mixed submitit helper script through the root facade"
tool = "helpers__run_cmd"
arguments = { args = [["{python_executable}", "{challenge_artifact_dir}/run_mixed_jobs.py", "{challenge_artifact_dir}"]], kwargs = { cwd = "{challenge_artifact_dir}" } }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["HELLO", "6", "8"]
files_nonempty = [
  "{challenge_artifact_dir}/mixed_summary.json",
  "{challenge_artifact_dir}/debug_files.txt",
  "{challenge_artifact_dir}/local_files.txt",
  "{challenge_artifact_dir}/auto_files.txt",
]
```
